name: IFCN Signatories Directory
description: International Fact-Checking Network verified signatories — organizations committed to non-partisanship, transparency, and methodological standards.
appUse: Primary lookup for verified fact-checking organizations worldwide

name: Poynter / IFCN Code of Principles
description: The 5-principle standard for ethical fact-checking. Used to evaluate any fact-checking source the user encounters.
appUse: Framework evidence for why certain sources are ranked higher

name: Google Fact Check Explorer
description: API-powered search engine for fact-checked claims globally. Uses ClaimReview schema markup.
appUse: Live claim lookup within Source Compare Mode and exercises

name: GDELT Project
description: Global media monitoring dataset covering 100+ languages. Tracks tone, themes, and source patterns.
appUse: Context data for media trend analysis and cross-referencing claims

name: Internet Archive / Wayback Machine
description: Digital library providing snapshots of web pages over time. Essential for verifying deleted or modified content.
appUse: Archive Replay mode — verify historical versions of claims and sources

name: Crossref
description: DOI registration agency providing metadata for 150M+ scholarly works. Primary academic source discovery tool.
appUse: Research sourcing API for all 3 MVPs — looking up peer-reviewed evidence

name: OpenAlex
description: Open catalog of scholarly papers, authors, institutions, and concepts. 250M+ works indexed.
appUse: Scholar discovery and citation network analysis across all MVPs

name: Misbar
description: Arabic-language fact-checking organization. IFCN signatory covering Middle East and North Africa.
appUse: Arabic fact-check lookups for Egyptian context claims

name: Fatabyyano
description: Award-winning Arabic fact-checking platform based in Jordan. Covers MENA region misinformation.
appUse: Secondary Arabic fact-check source for regional claims

name: WHO Mental Health
description: World Health Organization mental health topic page. Global authority on mental health definitions and guidelines.
appUse: Primary reference for mental health term definitions and educational content

name: Egypt Ministry of Health and Population (MoHP)
description: Egyptian Ministry of Health mental health portal. Local authority for mental health resources and crisis services.
appUse: Local Egyptian mental health resources, crisis contact, and service directory

name: Egypt GSMHAT
description: General Secretariat of Mental Health and Addiction Treatment. Operates the 16328 helpline.
appUse: Crisis contact source — 16328 helpline operator

name: NIMH (National Institute of Mental Health)
description: Lead US federal agency for research on mental disorders. Largest funder of mental health research globally.
appUse: Evidence-based disorder descriptions and research summaries

name: Al-Azhar
description: The oldest and most significant institution of Sunni Islamic learning. Reference body for religious guidance in Egypt.
appUse: Moderation source — official religious scholarly reference

name: Al-Azhar Observatory for Combating Extremism
description: Al-Azhar's dedicated observatory monitoring and countering extremist rhetoric in multiple languages.
appUse: Reference for identifying extremist content patterns and moderation guidelines

name: Dar al-Ifta Egypt
description: Official fatwa institution of Egypt. Provides religious guidance through qualified scholars. Hotline: 107.
appUse: Moderation source — official religious consultation and guidance resource

name: APA Division 36 (Society for Psychology of Religion and Spirituality)
description: APA's official division for the scientific study of religion and spirituality in psychological research.
appUse: Academic framework source for psychology-of-religion approach

name: Harvard Human Flourishing Program
description: Interdisciplinary research program studying human flourishing including religious/spiritual dimensions.
appUse: Research evidence for positive religious coping and flourishing connection

name: Semantic Scholar
description: AI-powered free research tool. 200M+ papers indexed with open API. Full abstracts and many full-text PDFs available.
appUse: Free alternative to paid databases — full paper search with AI summaries

name: PubMed Central (PMC)
description: NIH full-text archive of biomedical and life sciences literature. 100% free access to millions of peer-reviewed articles.
appUse: Free full-text mental health research papers — no paywall

name: CORE (Open Access Research)
description: World's largest aggregator of open access research. 300M+ papers. All free with open API.
appUse: Massive free paper discovery — aggregates all open access repositories globally

name: DOAJ (Directory of Open Access Journals)
description: Community-curated directory of 20,000+ open access journals. Quality-controlled, no predatory journals.
appUse: Verify if a journal is legitimate open access — anti-predatory-journal tool

name: Unpaywall
description: Browser extension + API that finds free full-text versions of paywalled papers. 30M+ free articles indexed.
appUse: Bypass paywalls legally — finds free versions of any paper with DOI

name: PsyArXiv
description: Free preprint server for psychological sciences. Access latest research before journal publication.
appUse: Free psychology research — preprints on mental health, cognition, behavior

name: Europe PMC
description: Free full-text biomedical literature search. 42M+ abstracts, 9M+ full-text articles. Includes preprints.
appUse: Free alternative to PubMed with more full-text access

name: ClinicalTrials.gov
description: Database of 450,000+ clinical studies worldwide. Free access to study protocols, results, and methodology.
appUse: Verify if a treatment claim has actual clinical trial evidence

name: Google Scholar
description: Free search across scholarly literature. Links to free full-text versions when available.
appUse: General-purpose free academic search — finds open access versions of papers

name: Sunnah.com
description: Comprehensive free hadith database. Searchable collections of Bukhari, Muslim, Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, and more.
appUse: Free hadith verification — search and verify any prophetic tradition

name: Quran.com
description: Free Quran text with translations, tafsir (exegesis), and audio. Multiple scholarly translations available.
appUse: Free Quran verification — check any verse reference with scholarly context

name: Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy
description: Free peer-reviewed encyclopedia of philosophy. Covers epistemology, logic, ethics, cognitive science.
appUse: Free reference for logical fallacies, epistemology, and critical thinking frameworks

name: Media Bias/Fact Check
description: Independent online media outlet that rates media sources for factual accuracy and political bias.
appUse: Check bias rating of any news source — free media literacy tool

name: Bad News Game
description: Cambridge University inoculation game teaching manipulation tactics. Scientifically validated in multiple studies.
appUse: Reference source for the EAL DeepReal Arena game methodology — inoculation theory

name: Psychology Today
description: Free accessible psychology articles written by licensed professionals. Therapist directory included.
appUse: Free mental health education — expert-written accessible articles
